title: thymus striatus subsp. acicularis
intro: Thymus striatus subsp. acicularis, also known as Acicular Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus genus, characterized by its small, needle-like leaves and aromatic properties. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe and calm the digestive system, as well as reduce stress and anxiety. The health benefits of Acicular Thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antiviral properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Acicular Thyme include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to create topical applications, such as creams and salves, or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
